executiveSummary: Earl's Performance Plumbing has clearly had professional SEO work done in the past -- 159 pages in your sitemap, LocalBusiness schema, branded email, 30+ service pages, and 40+ location pages. That's a serious investment. But the site is coasting on work that was done years ago, and it's starting to show.

The most concerning finding: your blog has been dead since March 2019 -- nearly 7 years without a single new post. Your reviews page exists but appears empty -- no visible reviews despite claiming 3,543 in schema. And you have 40+ video testimonials buried at /reviews/videos/ that are getting zero organic traffic because they're not on YouTube and have no video schema.

The foundation is strong. But a dormant blog, broken reviews page, and buried video content are vulnerabilities a competitor could exploit. Any plumbing company in the Chico/Redding market that starts publishing monthly content and actively managing their review presence will start overtaking you in local search.

assessment.title: Website Analysis -- earlsplumbing.net
points: 159 pages indexed in sitemap -- extensive content foundation across services and locations.
Built on WordPress with GeneratePress theme and Elementor -- functional but heavy.
LocalBusiness schema with AggregateRating (4.9/5, 3,543 reviews) -- strong structured data.
Branded email (contact@earlsplumbing.net) -- professional and credible.
30+ individual service pages covering leak detection, re-pipes, filtration, septic, wells, and more.
40+ location-specific pages across 8 counties -- excellent geographic coverage.
Google Analytics (GA4) and Google Tag Manager both installed.
Blog has been dead since March 2019 -- nearly 7 years without new content.

assessment.title: Content & SEO Gaps
points: Reviews page framework exists but no visible review content renders -- 3,543 reviews claimed in schema but not displayed.
40+ video testimonials at /reviews/videos/ are buried and invisible -- no YouTube channel, no video schema.
Blog last updated March 2019 with off-topic posts like 'Six Great Travel Ideas' and 'Seven Easy Ways to Eat Healthy.'
No FAQ schema on existing FAQ accordion content -- missing rich snippet opportunities.
Location pages are formulaic with minimal city-specific content -- Paradise page doesn't mention Camp Fire rebuilding.
Orphan page (/elementor-5606/) indexed -- Elementor test page publicly visible.
4 web font families loaded (Roboto, Roboto Slab, Poppins, Playfair Display) -- excessive for page speed.
Only Facebook linked -- no Instagram, YouTube, Yelp, or Nextdoor despite having video content.

assessment.title: Competitive Landscape
points: Chico/Redding plumbing market is competitive -- companies investing in SEO now will pull ahead.
Your 159-page content foundation gives you a massive head start over competitors building from scratch.
The Camp Fire recovery in Paradise created lasting demand for plumbing services -- your Paradise page should leverage this.
Video testimonials are an untapped competitive advantage -- no competitors in the area appear to have this asset.

findings.finding: Dead since March 2019 -- zero fresh content for nearly 7 years
Reviews page broken/empty despite claiming 3,543 in schema -- potential Google penalty
40+ video testimonials buried with no YouTube channel or video schema
FAQ accordion content exists but no FAQPage schema deployed
40+ location pages with thin, templated content lacking city-specific details
Elementor bloat + 4 font families -- likely poor mobile Core Web Vitals
Only Facebook linked -- missing Instagram, YouTube, Yelp, Nextdoor
LocalBusiness with AggregateRating present -- strong foundation

strategy.title: Phase 1: Revive & Fix (Months 1-2)
actions: Fix the reviews page -- integrate a review widget to pull Google/Yelp reviews with proper Review schema.
Add FAQ schema to all existing FAQ accordion content across service pages.
Create a YouTube channel and upload existing 40+ video testimonials -- free traffic source.
Clean up orphan pages (remove /elementor-5606/ and consolidate duplicate referral/employment pages).
Add unique content to location pages -- reference local landmarks, community events, and city-specific issues.
Optimize page speed -- reduce font families from 4 to 2, audit Elementor CSS/JS output.

strategy.title: Phase 2: Content Revival (Months 3-5)
actions: Relaunch blog with 2-4 posts/month: 'Winter pipe freeze prevention Chico CA,' 'Well water testing Oroville,' 'Paradise CA plumber after Camp Fire.'
Add Instagram and YouTube links to site navigation and footer.
Build Nextdoor business profile for local community recommendations.
Create emergency-specific landing pages: 'Emergency plumber Chico' and '24 hour plumber Redding.'
Update coupon page and ensure seasonal promotions are current.

strategy.title: Phase 3: Sustain & Grow (Months 6-12)
actions: Google Ads targeting high-intent plumbing keywords during peak seasons.
Video content: project walkthroughs, plumbing tips, seasonal maintenance guides.
Email marketing to existing customers for seasonal reminders and referral incentives.
Monthly reporting on traffic, rankings, lead generation, and review growth.
Competitor monitoring to maintain search position.

pricingIntro: Earl's Performance Plumbing has the largest content foundation of any plumber website I've audited in this market -- 159 pages is a massive asset. The strategy isn't about building from scratch -- it's about reviving what you have, fixing technical gaps, and restarting the content engine.
about: Ridge Cell Repair LLC is a California-based digital marketing and business analysis firm specializing in helping service-industry businesses grow their online presence and generate more leads. We focus on practical, results-driven strategies rather than flashy marketing that doesn't convert.
Our approach is data-first: we research your market, identify what your competitors are doing (and not doing), and build a strategy that delivers real business outcomes.
What sets us apart is that we treat every client like a partner, not an account number. You get direct access to the people doing the work -- no layers of account managers.
nextSteps: You've built something that most plumbing companies would envy -- 159 pages, video testimonials, branded email, multi-county coverage. The issue isn't the foundation, it's the maintenance. Let's get the engine running again.
